title: Missing 'Asking for Options' intervention category
description: The framing doc identifies 6 categories of human intervention. The guide covers 5 but omits category 6: requesting analysis before deciding. This is a distinct collaborative pattern that differentiates the guide from command-and-control framing.

title: Data section buries the guide's strongest claim
description: The concluding 'What the Data Shows' section reads as a methodology appendix. The hooks-vs-prompts finding (the guide's most important takeaway) is buried. The bullet list repeats numbers already in the text.

title: No explanation of beads/issue-tracking integration
description: The guide mentions beads in passing but never explains what it is, why it matters, or how it interacts with the harness. Readers without beads context will miss why premature closure is the #1 anti-pattern.

title: No harness developer/customization guidance
description: The synthesis has 6 developer recommendations. The guide omits them all. Many practitioners will customize the harness. At minimum, the agent permissions fix (#15, highest-ROI) should be included.

title: 14% failure rate drops 'or degraded output' qualifier
description: The guide says 'About 14% of agents fail outright' but the synthesis says '14% failed outright or produced significantly degraded output.' The qualifier matters for accuracy.

title: '15-50 agents' per T3 session range not verifiable
description: The guide claims 'A typical T3 session launches 15-50 agents' but the synthesis only provides aggregate counts (~155 across 16 sessions). The per-session range is not stated in any research document.

title: Intro paragraphs 2-3 could be tighter
description: Opening paragraph is vivid. Paragraphs 2-3 could combine into one tighter paragraph that goes straight from problem to model.

title: Probing section lacks actionable takeaway
description: Section has good quotes but no guidance on when/why to adopt probing behavior. Contrast with the advisory-notes trap which ends with a concrete rule of thumb.

title: Scope gaps section only shows expansions, not reductions
description: Synthesis shows 8 scope-control instances including reductions. Guide only shows expansions. Adding one reduction example ('I actually just want this workflow to have been this research step') shows scope control goes both ways.

title: Anti-patterns lack severity labels
description: The synthesis ranks anti-patterns by severity (Severity 1-3). The guide presents them at the same structural level without severity signals. Readers can't tell which are urgent vs merely annoying.

title: Defensive closing paragraph
description: Final italicized paragraph repeats 'actual' three times. The guide's credibility is self-evident from its content. Either cut or soften to one sentence.

title: '~155 agents... alone' implies known counts for other worktrees
description: The word 'alone' implies other worktrees had additional quantified agents, but they weren't counted.

title: '0% hook bypass rate' based on limited sample
description: The 0% claim is primarily from one session (S12). Presentation implies robust sample size.

title: Missing 'Asking for Options' intervention category
resolution: Added 'Requesting analysis before deciding' subsection with 3 quotes from framing doc

title: Data section buries the guide's strongest claim
resolution: Restructured as 'The Strongest Signal' — leads with hooks-vs-prompts finding, cut bullet list, ends on actionable implication

title: No explanation of beads/issue-tracking integration
resolution: Added 'Issue Tracking' subsection in Core Model explaining beads and why closure verification matters

title: No harness developer/customization guidance
resolution: Added 'Customizing the Harness' section with top 3 developer recommendations: permissions, verification, hooks

title: 14% failure rate drops 'or degraded output' qualifier
resolution: Changed to 'fail outright or produce significantly degraded output'

title: '15-50 agents' per T3 session range not verifiable
resolution: Replaced with verifiable aggregate: '~155 agents across 16 sessions in one worktree'
